articles.summary: AI is speeding things up, but all that new code creates a bottleneck — who's verifying the quality and security? Don't let unverified code stall your merges.

articles.title: GOOGLE PLANS TO RELEASE GEMINI 3 PRO PREVIEW IN NOVEMBER
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3 Pro preview model has appeared in VertexAI code, labeled '11-2025', suggesting a November launch. The timeline matches prior testing activity observed in AI Studio. Google has not issued any official statements about the launch.

articles.title: OPENAI ISN'T YET WORKING TOWARD AN IPO, CFO SAYS
articles.summary: OpenAI's Chief Financial Officer, Sarah Friar, said the company is not planning to IPO in the short term. It will instead prioritize growth and R&D over profitability. OpenAI is hoping the government will support its offers to increase data center capacity by helping to guarantee the financing for the chips behind its deals. The depreciation rates of AI chips are uncertain, making it more expensive for companies to raise the debt needed to buy them. Friar claims OpenAI could reach profitability on very healthy gross margins if it weren't seeking to invest so aggressively.

articles.title: GOOGLE GEMINI'S DEEP RESEARCH CAN LOOK INTO YOUR EMAILS, DRIVE, AND CHATS
articles.summary: Google Gemini's "Deep Research" now uses Gmail, Drive, and Chat data for AI-generated reports. The feature can create detailed research plans and reports, integrating personal workspace data with web searches. Currently available on desktop, Google plans to roll out mobile access soon.

articles.title: SEMANTIC SEARCH FOR CURSOR'S CODING AGENT
articles.summary: Cursor's coding agent improved performance by incorporating custom semantic search over codebases, leading to higher accuracy, fewer user iterations, and better retention of code changes.

articles.title: THOUGHTS BY A NON-ECONOMIST ON AI AND ECONOMICS
YOU DON'T NEED 10 DIFFERENT AI TOOLS TO DEPLOY AN AGENT
articles.summary: It only takes 1 platform to fast track agentic AI. OutSystems Agent Workbench [11] provides no-code tools, security guardrails, and automated quality reviews so you can deliver human-AI collaboration without the sprawl. Bosch, Heineken, and EDP are already using it to

articles.title: build agentic AI systems. Build with Agent Workbench
ENABLING TRILLION-PARAMETER MODELS ON AWS EFA
articles.summary: Perplexity published code enabling trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts models to run efficiently across multiple AWS servers for the first time, opening up less advanced data centers to AI inference. The challenge is that large models, even many open weights models, are too large to fit on single servers, but AWS' networking hardware was too slow for the constant inter-server communication these models require.

articles.title: STRUCTURED OUTPUT IN GEMINI API
articles.summary: Google enhanced the Gemini API with expanded JSON Schema support and improved property ordering, making structured outputs more reliable for use in multi-agent workflows and database tasks.

articles.title: PINTEREST CEO TOUTS OPEN SOURCE AI: ‘TREMENDOUS PERFORMANCE' WITH REDUCED COSTS
articles.summary: Pinterest plans to move forward with open source models for its various use cases. They come at a fraction of the cost of the larger model providers, and they deliver value to users effectively. The company will be observing user feedback to see if people actually want AI shopping agents before implementing them. The company is planning to roll out personalized boards curated with AI.

articles.title: SNAP AND PERPLEXITY SIGN $400 MILLION DEAL TO PUT AI SEARCH DIRECTLY IN SNAPCHAT
articles.summary: Perplexity, an AI search engine, will appear directly in Snapchat sometime in early 2026. It will become a prominent part of Snapchat's 'chat' interface. Perplexity is paying Snap $400 million for the integration. Snap may pursue similar partnerships with other AI companies.

articles.summary: 100K+ developers build APIs, secure data, and scale instantly, without touching a single server or config file. See why Xano is the

OPENAI CALLS FOR $1T INVESTMENT TO SECURE US AI LEADERSHIP
articles.summary: The US risks losing its AI leadership due to energy shortages, threatening economic growth and technological advances.

articles.title: HOW TWO SILICON VALLEY A-LISTERS BECAME BILLIONAIRES BY REMAKING CUSTOMER SERVICE WITH AI
articles.summary: Bret Taylor and Clay Bavor started Sierra, a $10 billion startup that builds AI customer service agents, in 2023.

articles.title: A SHORT LESSON IN SIMPLER PROMPTS
articles.summary: You can build much shorter, more effective prompts by working with AI models instead of fighting them and balancing AI with plain old engineering.
